posts.hook: 20/20 vision doesn't mean you can see 20/20. Here's what that number actually measures.
posts.caption:
20/20 is not perfect vision.

It's average vision.

20/20 means: at 20 feet, you see what a typical eye sees at 20 feet.

Some people see 20/15. Or 20/10.

And 20/20 tells you nothing about:
- Night vision
- Color perception
- Peripheral vision
- Depth perception
- Contrast sensitivity

Free full vision assessment: axeo.vision

#VisionMyth #2020Vision #EyeHealth #Axeo #HealthTok #VisionTest #Optometry

posts.hook: How to read a Snellen chart result — and what the numbers actually mean.
posts.caption:
The Snellen chart is 160 years old. Here's how to actually read it.

The format: Distance/Distance

20/20 = You see at 20ft what a typical eye sees at 20ft ✓
20/40 = You see at 20ft what a typical eye sees at 40ft (worse)
20/10 = You see at 20ft what a typical eye sees at 10ft (better than average)

Legal driving minimum in most countries: 20/40

Fighter pilot requirement: 20/20 uncorrected

Hawks reportedly see: ~20/2

Most adults: 20/20 to 20/25 with correction

Test yours free: axeo.vision

#SnellenChart #VisionTest #EyeHealth #Axeo #AxeoVision #VisionScience #Optometry

posts.hook: 5 things that can make your vision worse that have nothing to do with your prescription.
posts.caption:
You can have a perfect prescription and still see poorly.

Factors that impair vision beyond refractive error:

1. Dehydration — tear film breaks up, blurs vision
2. Sleep deprivation — reduces accommodation speed
3. Screen glare — creates a luminance veil over fine detail
4. High stress — pupil dilation from cortisol reduces depth of field
5. Magnesium deficiency — linked to increased light sensitivity

These are all modifiable.

Track your vision weekly to notice changes early: axeo.vision

#VisionHealth #EyeHealth #Axeo #NutritionEyes #DigitalEyeStrain #Optometry #EyeScience

posts.hook: 20/20 is not the top of the vision scale. It's the midpoint.
posts.caption:
Vision acuity scale:

20/200 → Legal blindness
20/100 → Can't read a standard billboard
20/70  → Difficulty with newspaper text
20/40  → Legal driving minimum
20/25  → Most corrected adults
20/20  → 'Normal' — the baseline, not the ceiling
20/15  → Better than average
20/10  → Exceptional acuity
20/5   → Theoretical max for the human eye

Most people think 20/20 is perfect.
It's average.

Test yours free: axeo.vision
posts.visual_notes: Infographic: vertical acuity scale with colored tiers. Dark background, teal for 20/20 line highlighted as midpoint. Clean Axeo brand design.

posts.hook: I thought I had 20/20 vision. Found out I was wrong, and here's how I checked it for free.
posts.caption:
r/optometry or r/EyeCare

Background: I've worn contact lenses for years. Had my prescription, thought I was fine. Never questioned the '20/20 corrected' notation on my prescription.

Last week I was curious after reading about contrast sensitivity and decided to take the free test battery on axeo.vision (no signup required, browser-based, 5 minutes total).

Results:
- Snellen: 20/25 (mild, but slightly worse than I thought)
- Contrast sensitivity: Below average for my age range — which actually explained why evening driving had felt harder lately
- Color vision: Normal

None of this was detected at my last annual exam because: (1) my optometrist uses a physical Snellen chart at fixed distance, and (2) contrast sensitivity wasn't tested at all.

I went back and specifically asked about my contrast sensitivity — my optometrist confirmed it and mentioned early cataract changes (I'm 38).

The tests are free at axeo.vision and took maybe 6 minutes total. Worth knowing your actual baseline.
posts.visual_notes: First-person account format. Educational, detailed, community-appropriate. Soft promotion through personal experience.
